reasoning:
The strict legal prohibitions and severe punitive measures (imprisonment and deportation) in the UAE make political and public protests exceptionally rare. The reference baseline for the 360 days leading up to October 26, 2025, is exactly 0.000, confirming that ACLED recorded zero protests in that entire year. Because the baseline is 0, any single protest (1 or more) within the 30 days before each resolution date would force a positive resolution. 

For the first date (2025-11-02), the 30-day window is Oct 3 to Nov 2. As of Oct 26, web searches indicate no protests have occurred, leaving just 7 days in the window. The probability is set at the minimum allowed threshold (0.05). For the latter two dates (2025-11-25 and 2026-01-24), there are full 30-day windows in the future. Given occasional rare anomalies (like the brief July 2024 Bangladesh-related protests or occasional labor union strikes with public demonstrations), there is a tiny but non-zero chance of a protest occurring. To reflect horizon uncertainty, the probabilities are marginally scaled up to 0.06 and 0.07, honoring the base rate while acknowledging the inherent unpredictability slightly further into the future.
